title: sherbournia hapalophylla subsp. hapalophylla
intro: Sherbournia hapalophylla subsp. hapalophylla is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, malaria, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Sherbournia hapalophylla subsp. hapalophylla may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its safety and efficacy.
